title: Air China Cargo Expands Operations at Glasgow Prestwick

sections.heading: Daily Chengdu service
sections.content: Air China Cargo has increased its Glasgow Prestwick to Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport service to a daily operation after demand rose on the route. The service had been running four times a week until the latest increase. Air Cargo News reported that the link largely carries e-commerce traffic from China while providing exporters in Scotland with a direct connection into western China.

sections.heading: Trade flows through Prestwick
sections.content: In the opposite direction, the route carries Scottish exports including salmon, seafood and whisky. Glasgow Prestwick said the new daily frequency brings the airport's total number of scheduled cargo flights direct to China to 15 and said those trade links are expected to facilitate more than £250m in cross-border trade during 2026. The airport also noted last month that it had handled 25 million parcels through its Terminal E facility.

sections.heading: Why the airport sees the route as strategic
sections.content: Prestwick chief executive Ian Forgie said the move to daily flights reflected strong demand for direct cargo connectivity between the UK and China and reinforced the airport's role as a cargo gateway for international carriers. Colin Dai, Prestwick's regional director for Asia, described Chengdu as one of western China's most important logistics hubs and said daily connectivity would give Scottish exporters faster and more reliable access to major regional markets while improving inbound links from a major manufacturing region.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Air China Cargo ramps up Glasgow Prestwick operation
